channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷🇮🇱 Iran and Israel Halt Strikes After Trump Appeal
item.description: Iran and Israel said they halted attacks on each other after US President Donald Trump called for an immediate ceasefire, though Tehran warned it would resume strikes if Israel continued hitting Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: 🇨🇳📦 China Exports Surge on Chip Demand and Front-Loaded Orders
item.description: China's export growth likely strengthened in May as companies rushed orders ahead of energy price pressures from the Gulf conflict, while global semiconductor demand remained robust, a Reuters poll showed.

item.title: 🇮🇳📈 India Inflation Rises to 4% on Food and Fuel Costs
item.description: India's inflation likely rose to the Reserve Bank's 4% target in May, driven by higher vegetable prices and fuel costs linked to the US-Israel conflict with Iran, a Reuters poll indicated.

item.title: ⚔️🛑 Iran and Israel Pull Back After Fierce Exchange of Attacks
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, an Iranian-backed militia, is complicating efforts to end the war in Iran as both sides stepped back from further escalation following intense strikes.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Begins Large-Scale Delivery of Gallium Chips for 6G Network
item.description: Beijing is deploying millions of third-generation semiconductors using silicon-based technology for a planned space-ground 6G communications network, advancing its strategic tech capabilities amid export controls.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Hits Back at Beijing's "Cognitive Warfare" After Coastguard Patrols
item.description: Taiwan's defense minister accused Beijing of "cognitive warfare" after Chinese coastguard vessels patrolled waters around the island, saying the operation aimed to assert jurisdiction over areas Beijing does not control.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Rejects Ukrainian, European Peace Initiatives, Says Battlefield Will Decide War
item.description: Senior Russian officials effectively rejected recent Ukrainian and European proposals aimed at restarting peace talks, signaling that Moscow sees military gains as the path to resolution.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Polish Concerns Over Ukraine EU Entry "Solved," Enlargement Chief Says
item.description: The EU's enlargement chief Marta Kos said concerns raised by Poland over Ukraine's EU accession have been addressed at the working level, potentially smoothing the path for Kyiv's membership bid.

item.title: 🇹🇷 NATO's Ankara Summit: A Reckoning for the Alliance
item.description: As NATO allies confront uncertainties on multiple fronts, the upcoming Ankara Summit may become the alliance's most important meeting of the decade, with Turkey playing host amid shifting regional dynamics.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV warns of 'profound crisis' in historic address to Spanish parliament
item.description: In his first speech to a national parliament, Pope Leo XIV called for respect for migrants and international law, warning that escalating conflicts and polarization have pushed the world into a deep crisis.

item.title: 🇰🇵 China's Xi Vows to Work With North Korea Against 'Hegemony'
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping pledged to strengthen cooperation with North Korea in what North Korean media described as a joint effort to counter "hegemony," signaling closer ties amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines Verifies Reports of Deaths After Magnitude 7.8 Quake
item.description: Philippine authorities were verifying reports of at least five deaths after a powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck, causing significant damage in affected areas.

item.title: 🍎 Apple WWDC Kicks Off as Investors Watch AI Plans for Siri
item.description: Apple's annual developer conference began with investors eager to learn how the company plans to integrate artificial intelligence into Siri and other products amid intensifying competition.

item.title: 🌍 WHO's Tedros: Uganda Should Reconsider Congo Border Closure Over Ebola
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus urged Uganda to reconsider its border closure with Congo, warning that restricting movement could hamper Ebola response efforts.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon: Israel Has Bombed Lebanon Nearly 3,500 Times During Ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's defence minister said Israel has carried out nearly 3,500 airstrikes and hundreds of controlled explosions since the US-announced ceasefire in April, despite the truce agreement.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Says China Coast Guard Patrols to Its East Are 'Provocative Act'
item.description: Taiwan's defence minister called Chinese coast guard patrols to the east of the island a "provocative act" and said the military would coordinate with the coast guard in responding.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Chinese Military Hovered as Global Executives Flocked to Taiwan Tech Show
item.description: Chinese military aircraft operated near Taiwan as global technology executives gathered for the island's premier tech show, underscoring the tensions surrounding the semiconductor powerhouse.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea's Lee: Success in Business Should Be Shared, Plans Surplus Tax
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung said business success should be shared with society and announced plans for a surplus tax on corporations, signaling a shift in economic policy.

item.title: 💰 Major Gulf Markets Drop Amid Fresh Middle East Tensions
item.description: Most stock markets in the Gulf ended lower as renewed conflict in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment, with escalating regional tensions driving risk aversion.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Bennett Slams Netanyahu for Normalizing Periodic Attacks From Iran
item.description: Former Israeli Prime Minister Naftali Bennett criticized Netanyahu for what he called normalizing periodic Iranian attacks, questioning the government's security strategy.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Knesset Passes Law to Deduct Terror Compensation From PA Tax Revenues
item.description: Israel's parliament passed legislation allowing the deduction of compensation for terror victims from Palestinian Authority tax revenues, targeting payments to families of attackers.

item.title: ✈️ Franco-German-Spanish FCAS Fighter Program Dead: Reports
item.description: Reports indicate the Future Combat Air System (FCAS), a joint fighter program between France, Germany, and Spain, has collapsed amid disagreements over workshare and technology transfer.

item.title: ⚖️ Ukraine's Former Supreme Court Chief Justice Jailed for Graft
item.description: Ukraine's former Supreme Court chief justice was sentenced to prison on corruption charges, marking a significant step in the country's anti-corruption efforts during wartime.

item.title: ☢️ IAEA Calls on Iran to Re-Engage as West Pressures With Resolution
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency urged Iran to re-engage with nuclear inspections as Western powers pushed a resolution increasing pressure on Tehran over its nuclear program.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Seeks Sway Over Peace Talks After Brief Iran Fight, Defying Trump
item.description: Israel conducted a brief strike against Iran despite Trump's push for diplomacy, seeking to maintain influence over ongoing peace negotiations and regional security arrangements.
